root_text.sn47.19:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sumbhesu viharati sedakaṁ nāma sumbhānaṁ nigamo.
root_text.sn47.19:1.3: “bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, caṇḍālavaṁsiko caṇḍālavaṁsaṁ ussāpetvā medakathālikaṁ antevāsiṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn47.19:1.4: ‘ehi tvaṁ, samma medakathālike, caṇḍālavaṁsaṁ abhiruhitvā mama uparikhandhe tiṭṭhāhī’ti.
root_text.sn47.19:1.5: ‘Evaṁ, ācariyā’ti kho, bhikkhave, medakathālikā antevāsī caṇḍālavaṁsikassa paṭissutvā caṇḍālavaṁsaṁ abhiruhitvā ācariyassa uparikhandhe aṭṭhāsi.
root_text.sn47.19:1.6: Atha kho, bhikkhave, caṇḍālavaṁsiko medakathālikaṁ antevāsiṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.19:1.7: ‘tvaṁ, samma medakathālike, mamaṁ rakkha, ahaṁ taṁ rakkhissāmi.
root_text.sn47.19:1.8: Evaṁ mayaṁ aññamaññaṁ guttā aññamaññaṁ rakkhitā sippāni ceva dassessāma, lābhañca lacchāma, sotthinā ca caṇḍālavaṁsā orohissāmā’ti.
root_text.sn47.19:1.9: Evaṁ vutte, bhikkhave, medakathālikā antevāsī caṇḍālavaṁsikaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn47.19:1.11: Tvaṁ, ācariya, attānaṁ rakkha, ahaṁ attānaṁ rakkhissāmi.
root_text.sn47.19:1.12: Evaṁ mayaṁ attaguttā attarakkhitā sippāni ceva dassessāma, lābhañca lacchāma, sotthinā ca caṇḍālavaṁsā orohissāmā’”ti.
root_text.sn47.19:2.1: “So tattha ñāyo”ti bhagavā etadavoca, “yathā medakathālikā antevāsī ācariyaṁ avoca.
root_text.sn47.19:2.4: Attānaṁ, bhikkhave, rakkhanto paraṁ rakkhati, paraṁ rakkhanto attānaṁ rakkhati.
root_text.sn47.19:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, attānaṁ rakkhanto paraṁ rakkhati?
root_text.sn47.19:3.3: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, attānaṁ rakkhanto paraṁ rakkhati.
root_text.sn47.19:3.4: Kathañca, bhikkhave, paraṁ rakkhanto attānaṁ rakkhati?
root_text.sn47.19:3.6: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, paraṁ rakkhanto attānaṁ rakkhati.
root_text.sn47.19:3.9: Attānaṁ, bhikkhave, rakkhanto paraṁ rakkhati, paraṁ rakkhanto attānaṁ rakkhatī”ti.

translation_text.sn47.19:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sumbhas, near the town of the Sumbhas named Sedaka.
translation_text.sn47.19:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn47.19:1.3: “Once upon a time, mendicants, a pole acrobat of the corpse-workers set up the bamboo pole of his tribe and said to his apprentice Medakathālikā,
translation_text.sn47.19:1.4: ‘Come now, dear Medakathālikā, climb up the bamboo pole and stand on my shoulders.’
translation_text.sn47.19:1.5: ‘Yes, tutor,’ she replied. She climbed up the bamboo pole and stood on her tutor’s shoulders.
translation_text.sn47.19:1.6: Then the pole acrobat of the corpse-workers said to Medakathālikā,
translation_text.sn47.19:1.7: ‘You look after me, dear Medakathālikā, and I’ll look after you.
translation_text.sn47.19:1.8: That’s how, guarding and looking after each other, we’ll display our skill, collect our fee, and get down safely from the bamboo pole.’
translation_text.sn47.19:1.9: When he said this, Medakathālikā said to her teacher,
translation_text.sn47.19:1.11: You should look after yourself, and I’ll look after myself.
translation_text.sn47.19:1.12: That’s how, guarding and looking after ourselves, we’ll display our skill, collect our fee, and get down safely from the bamboo pole.’
translation_text.sn47.19:2.1: That’s the way,” said the Buddha. “It’s just as Medakathālikā said to her tutor.
translation_text.sn47.19:2.2: Thinking ‘I’ll look after myself,’ you should cultivate mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.19:2.3: Thinking ‘I’ll look after others,’ you should cultivate mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.19:2.4: Looking after yourself, you look after others; and looking after others, you look after yourself.
translation_text.sn47.19:3.1: And how do you look after others by looking after yourself?
translation_text.sn47.19:3.2: By development, cultivation, and practice of meditation.
translation_text.sn47.19:3.4: And how do you look after yourself by looking after others?
translation_text.sn47.19:3.5: By acceptance, harmlessness, love, and sympathy.
translation_text.sn47.19:3.7: Thinking ‘I’ll look after myself,’ you should cultivate mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.19:3.8: Thinking ‘I’ll look after others,’ you should cultivate mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.19:3.9: Looking after yourself, you look after others; and looking after others, you look after yourself.”
